title: Fermented Middle Eastern Padron Pepper Pilaf
description: A fluffy, Mediterraneanfriendly pilaf that pairs tangy fermented padron peppers with aromatic spices, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Rinse the fermented padron peppers, drain, and chop them into 1 cm pieces.
In a medium saucepan, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Stir in the ground cumin and coriander, cooking for another minute to toast the spices.
Add the rice, stirring to coat each grain with the oil and spices; toast for 2 minutes.
Mix in the chopped fermented padron peppers, ensuring they are well distributed.
Pour in the water or broth, bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed, about 15–18 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the pilaf rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork, then stir in the lemon zest and chopped parsley. Serve warm or refrigerate in airtight containers for meal prep.
